texto: Maybe I am getting too grown to want to sleep with Mama. But like a baby, I still have this smallness to my mind. I don't need her hands to convince me the world I can't see from under my blanket is real. I need her hands to do more than her words. Convince me the world I see outside it- ain't.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Connie Rose Porter: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Maybe I am getting too grown to want to sleep with Mama. But like a baby, I still have this small…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, fear, imani-all-mine pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de imani-all-mine e parents-and-children.
